### Post by xc3RnbFO8P on 2009-04-05
First **sudo dpkg --reconfigure xserver-xorg** 
then edit your xorg.conf

Section "Monitor"
Identifier "Generic"
Option "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
Identifier "Default Screen"
Device "intel"
Monitor "Generic"
DefaultDepth 24
Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
EndSection

Restart.

---

### Post by xc3RnbFO8P on 2009-04-07
In my laptop I have (searching **xorg intel**):
xserver-xorg-video-i740 (installed)
xserver-xorg-video-i810
xserver-xorg-video-intel (installed)
xserver-xorg-video-intel-dbg

In Synaptic Package Manager:
Settings> repositories> Ubuntu Software check all
Settings> repositories> Update> check all
Settings> repositories>Third Party software check all
Then reload
and in Terminal **sudo apt-get update**

### Post by roshanjose on 2009-04-07
how do i get the Xorg.0.log file??

---

### Post by nebileix on 2009-04-08
> **roshanjose said:**
> how do i get the Xorg.0.log file??

```
$ cat /var/log/Xorg.0.log
```

---

### Post by b1ackmai1er on 2009-04-08
I have the same chipset and 8.10 does not recognize my monitor.

I  looked up the details of the monitor using google and then put them in my xorg.conf file.

It will still not recognize my monitor by now it allows me to select different screen resolutions and refresh rates.

Extract of my xorg.conf file:

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"NEC MultiSync 6FG"
	VendorName	"NEC Technologies"
	ModelName	"NEC MultiSync 6FG"
	HorizSync	27-79
	VertRefresh	55-90
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"NEC MultiSync 6FG"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)

# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# Note that some configuration settings that could be done previously
# in this file, now are automatically configured by the server and settings
